whatsNewItems.title: Moved Hosting to Raspberry Pi 5
whatsNewItems.description: I moved the site and API to a Raspberry Pi 5 microserver (16GB RAM, 1TB NVMe) with Cloudflare Tunnel and registered domain papispictures.com for public access. I did this because it was easier to manage than Render for this project, gives me more control, and avoids potential monthly hosting costs.

whatsNewItems.title: Added Node.js + Express Backend
whatsNewItems.description: The site is no longer static-only. Services and 'What's new' cards, and recent pictures photos are now loaded from a Node.js + Express API at /api. This allows for dynamic content management and sets the stage for future features like a MongoDB-backed gallery and licensing system.

whatsNewItems.title: Navigation and Layout Cleanup
whatsNewItems.description: I cleaned up the frontend structure and fixed mobile navigation behavior. Fixed broken menu links.The page now uses a clearer section layout (welcome, about, updates, services), and the hamburger menu works reliably with anchor links. Website is currently a single page design where additional pages will be added as needed for future features like the gallery and licensing system.

whatsNewItems.title: Added Additional Content Sections
whatsNewItems.description: Added additional content in the form of an 'About section' and a 'What's New' section that can be used to provide notes on updates for the site and service changes, and promotions.

whatsNewItems.title: Next Up: Full Gallery and Licensing
whatsNewItems.description: Next step is a MongoDB-backed gallery system for dynamic photo/video management. Planned work includes search and filters, gallery creation (collections of photos that share a theme), tagging, and direct license purchases through the site.
